notes: Iteration 3 adds two new assertions replacing 'Build instructions mention setting up the Adams environment': 'Agent offers to compile' and 'Manual instructions are fallback only'. Total assertions increased from 26 to 28.
With-skill scored 28/28 (100%) — perfect score across all three evals. The iteration-2 failure (cache-and-reuse env setup) is now fixed.
Without-skill scored 2/28 (7.1%). Only vfosub-spring-force passes 2 assertions (c_sysfnc and c_errmes usage).
forbidden-call-trap without-skill dropped from 1/5 (20%) to 0/5 (0%). Iteration-2 had a helper using c_sysary; this run has no SDK utility calls at all.
The skill now achieves the maximum possible discriminating delta: with-skill 100% vs without-skill 7.1% = +92.9pp.
Build workflow framing change (agent offers to compile, manual is fallback) successfully addressed the iteration-2 failure. The SKILL.md restructuring had the intended effect.
vfosub-spring-force without-skill: correctly uses c_sysfnc('DX') and c_errmes — these reflect baseline LLM knowledge of Adams solver API from training data, but all SDK-specific patterns are missing.
